title: So whos up for a fan dub
display_title: So whos up for a fan dub
wikitext:
[]: So who's up for a fan dub?             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri Nov 14, 2008 7:16 am As some of the members of this forum site are already in on this I would like to say I have a decompiled version of Rebuild. I understand the real dub is about a year away but I cant wait and I'm sick of subs (also suck shit we got it before the US). So I know some people are objective the the English dub but lets try make it something everyone can enjoy. Who is in let me know and squabble amongst yourselves as to who is playing who and ill do the dubbing just make sure your lines all line up with the lips of the characters.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Fri Nov 14, 2008 9:47 am I would love to: Im intrested in doing Kaworu Gendou and touji

[]:             Quote Post by Maximilian » Fri Nov 14, 2008 10:33 am I would definitely be interested in doing it. I'd really do any male part, including Shinji and Kaworu, though my voice may be too deep for it (think how Heero Yuy in the English dub had a rather deep voice for his age). It might be a good idea to share sound clips as "auditions."

[]: Re: So Whos up for a fan dub.             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 14, 2008 12:37 pm Zeak wrote: also suck shit

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Nov 14, 2008 12:46 pm You're sick of subs? Sick of subs? Why so sick?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Nov 14, 2008 2:21 pm This better be for an abridged parody, or I guarantee it'll be pretty bad. Fan dubs usually suck as a rule.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 14, 2008 2:23 pm A lot of parody dubs suck, too, for that matter. (But when they work, they work well.)

[]:             Quote Post by RockManBalls » Fri Nov 14, 2008 2:54 pm Beware the subtitles that are floating around if you intend to go through with a project like that using them as a script. The NF one is iffy and Nyoro~n has as a mission statement to release consistent fail-subs. I have the R3 release of the movie which does have English subtitles. I'm torn on whether or not they're the ones used in festival showings, but they are definitely a step up from others. I've OCRed them using subrip. Niiiice. Rapidshare get: http://rapidshare.com/files/163798469/Evangelion_R3_Subtitles.srt.html

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Nov 14, 2008 3:16 pm RockManBalls wrote: I have the R3 release of the movie which does have English subtitles. I'm torn on whether or not they're the ones used in festival showings, but they are definitely a step up from others. I've OCRed them using subrip. Niiiice.

[]:             Quote Post by RockManBalls » Fri Nov 14, 2008 3:31 pm I hear ya. Still, I think they serve as a more literal translation than the others, who, for understandable reasons, took some liberties. For a project that would demand accuracy, it's a good reference.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Fri Nov 14, 2008 5:47 pm Definately would like to try this, especially if I can try for Shini again, as I didn't manage to get the part for the RE-take project.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Nov 15, 2008 1:27 am English-accented Shinji is seven degrees of win.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Nov 15, 2008 2:19 am Irish accented Touji anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Nov 15, 2008 2:53 am Now we need an Australian Kensuke and a Scottish Gendo, and this show is good to go.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 15, 2008 3:12 am Plus a Welsh Fuyutsuki and a Canadian Kaworu who says "aboot" (somewhere, somehow). The Seele people all have redneck accents, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Nov 15, 2008 4:24 am CorporalChaos wrote: English-accented Shinji is seven degrees of win.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 15, 2008 1:25 pm I wouldn't really have the time for this, other projects are keeping me busy enough. I guess it works since Asuka isn't even in 1.0 anyway, but Misato would be fun. Oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Nov 15, 2008 2:36 pm I can reprise Black Hyuga.
